user_query:
I'm a firefighter trying to modernize our station’s training materials ahead of a reliability/incident-readiness huddle. Using the files I’ve put in this workspace, please do a focused audit and prep a small briefing pack.

Inputs you must use:
- config/training_drill.yml — a YAML config listing our current training modules. Use the embedded reference_date for any age/staleness checks.
- doc/aars_template.md — our barebones After Action Review (AAR) template to be updated.

Tasks and required outputs:
1) Audit our training modules and produce out/audit_report.json with one object per module including:
   - id (string)
   - title (string)
   - last_reviewed (YYYY-MM-DD)
   - topic (one of: MAYDAY, ICS, PPE, Ladders, Hose) derived from id using this mapping:
       mayday -> MAYDAY; ics_201 -> ICS; ppe_doffing -> PPE; ladder_ops -> Ladders; hose_advance -> Hose
   - status (one of: current, stale, needs_research, stale_and_needs_research) computed as:
       • stale if (reference_date − last_reviewed) > 3 years
       • needs_research if source_org == "Unknown"
       • both true => stale_and_needs_research; neither => current
   - reasons (array of strings explaining which rule(s) triggered)
   The audit must be derived solely from config/training_drill.yml and be consistent with the above rules.

2) Perform web research and create research/sources.json with 3–5 credible, official references relevant to updating firefighter training. Requirements:
   - Use a search engine to find current, official guidance or reference pages from at least two of these organizations: NFPA, FEMA, USFA, NIOSH (FFFIPP), OSHA.
   - Each entry includes: title, organization, year (if available; else null), url, and topic (choose from MAYDAY, ICS, PPE, Ladders, Hose), plus a short 1–2 sentence relevance note.
   - At least two entries must map to topics that appear among modules flagged as stale or needs_research in out/audit_report.json.
   - Do not download large files; just capture the bibliographic metadata and a stable URL.

3) Prepare out/findings_and_actions.md with concise meeting notes for a 30‑minute planning huddle. Include:
   - A one‑paragraph summary of the audit (counts by status)
   - A bulleted action list: one action per module with status != current. Each action item must include the module id, its status, a one‑line next step, and reference at least one organization and topic from research/sources.json that would guide the update.
   - A final section listing open questions or dependencies.

4) Update the AAR template and save to out/aars_template_updated.md by starting from doc/aars_template.md and adding the following sections after the existing content:
   - "Lessons Learned"
   - "Training Gaps"
   - "References" — include at least two citation stubs derived from research/sources.json in the format: "- [Organization] — [Title] ([Year or n.d.])"

5) Draft a short email to our Training Chief and save to communications/email_to_training_chief.txt (<= 250 words). Include:
   - A subject line
   - A brief summary of what you audited and why
   - A bullet list of modules flagged for update with their statuses
   - A request to approve next steps and a quick sync
   - Explicit relative paths to out/audit_report.json and research/sources.json

Important notes:
- The staleness calculation must use reference_date from the YAML, not today’s date.
- The Internet step is essential: use a search engine to locate official pages; do not rely on unofficial blogs. Include the chosen URLs only in research/sources.json.
- Keep outputs self‑contained in the specified paths under the workspace.
- Make sure the action items in out/findings_and_actions.md are consistent with both out/audit_report.json and research/sources.json.

Deliverables to create:
- out/audit_report.json
- research/sources.json
- out/findings_and_actions.md
- out/aars_template_updated.md
- communications/email_to_training_chief.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
